description: BAC can keep rising after you stop drinking, and consuming alcohol on an empty stomach or being lighter in weight can increase BAC.
In heavy rain, leaving extra space is important because stopping distance increases and visibility may be reduced.
When passing a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights, you must slow down and pass with caution.
If you feel sick or unusually tired, do not drive until you are fit and alert, as it can affect your driving ability.
Scanning the road ahead helps detect hazards early, allowing you to plan and respond safely.
When sharing roads with heavy vehicles, you should allow extra time and space to ensure safety.
Towing beyond legal limits can result in fines or penalties.
A NO U-TURN sign indicates that making a U-turn at that location is prohibited.
You must not park within the restricted distance from an intersection without signs.
At children's crossings, pedestrians have priority when flags are displayed.
Random breath tests are used to deter and detect drink driving. If required, you must cooperate and provide a breath sample.
In a school zone, you must follow the lower speed limit displayed during the specified times.
When approaching a stop line at an intersection without a sign, you must stop at the line and give way.
In wet conditions, maintain a larger gap between vehicles to prevent accidents due to reduced traction and stopping distance.
Avoid tailgating trucks as it reduces visibility and stopping distance.
When workers are present, you must drive cautiously, even if no temporary speed sign is visible.
You may drive in a bus lane only if permitted by signs and during the specified times.
Only vehicles permitted by the sign may use loading zones; misuse can result in fines.
